controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday August 30, 2024 at 08:10

descriptivenote.p: Materials are all written in English.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of personal and travel diaries, academic writings, short story drafts, research and lecture notes, and correspondence. Topics covered in the collection are Gaddis Smith's early life, living in New Haven, marriage and family, aging, hobbies, running and fitness, archival and general research, attending Yale and Duke University, work at Yale University as a professor, chair of the Department of History, and directorship of the Yale University MacMillian Center for International and Area Studies (formerly known as Yale Center for International and Area Studies), international travel and politics, Iraq War, 9/11, Vietnam War protesting and arrest records, maritime history of US Great Lakes region and Atlantic Ocean, and Yale Alumni associations.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: George Gaddis Smith, mostly known as Gaddis Smith, was born December 9, 1932, in Newark, New Jersey and lived his early life in Summit, New Jersey where he attended and graduated Pingry School in 1950. Smith married Barclay Manierre in 1951. He attended Yale College and served as the Chairman for the Yale Daily News in 1953. He graduated from Yale College in 1954. He spent the next seven years teaching and pursuing his PhD in history at Duke University and returned to Yale to teach in 1961. He became Head (title formerly known as "Master") of Pierson College in 1972; he served in the position for nine years working closely with Yale President Kingman Brewster. Throughout the 1980s he taught courses on American history and foreign policy. Some of his students from this time went on to become public figures in the entertainment industry and US politics.

Smith published a variety of journal articles, opinion pieces, and academic books on American history, specifically cold war history and US foreign policy in the 20th century. At Yale, he chaired the Department of History and was the Director for Yale Center for International and Area Studies. In 2001, he began teaching a course on Yale University history and the US. This formed the basis of his book on the history of Yale University, published in 2023. Gaddis Smith passed away in his home from progressive supranuclear palsy in New Haven, CT on December 9, 2022.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research. Materials in Boxes 12-21, Series 1: Diaries and correspondence, are restricted until January 1, 2075.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright and other associated proprietary rights for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Gaddis Smith was transferred to Yale University in 2023. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact beinecke.library@yale.edu. Status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gaddis Smith papers. General Collection, Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Gaddis Smith papers are organized into three series: I. Diaries and correspondence, 1919-2014. II. Public talks and lectures, 1977-2007. III. Writings, 1972-2004.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related materials: Institute of International Studies, Yale University, records, RU 482; and MacMillan Center for International and Area Studies at Yale records, RU 465.

subject.part: Foreign policy of the United States

corpname.part: Yale Center for International and Area Studies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Majority diary entries, newspaper clippings, and correspondence to and from family members and colleagues dispersed throughout series. Diary entries cover Smith's early life, educational career, personal and professional life in New Haven, CT, international politics, travel, hobbies, aging, and reflections on major world events.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials in Boxes 12-21, Series I. Diaries and correpondence, are restricted until January 1, 2075.

did.unittitle: "¡SI A CHILE! ¡SI A PINOCHET!," loose material found in 1987-1993 notebook

accessrestrict.p: Materials in Boxes 12-21, Series I. Diaries and correpondence restricted unitl January 1, 2075.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series contains notes, drafts, and research related to lectures and talks Smith gave at Yale University, in New England area, and nationally related to US foreign policy, international politics, the history of Yale University, and maritime history.

did.unittitle: Fiction, Anglo-American Relations (1911-1921), American Diplomacy During WWII notes
Containment in East Asia, National War College
Christian conscience and Nicaragua sermon, Trinity Church on the Green

title.part: In drawing a line in gulf, will we be the one to trip?

did.unittitle: NY Times book review, Anthony Eden by Robert Rhodes James
Antecedents of the USS Stark affair, abdication of LBJ
Comments on Scholzinger and Ribuffo papers
Presidential election and the next four years, North End Club, New Haven
Glasnot, Diplomatic History, and Post-Cold War Agenda, Yale Journal of World Affairs
The past 50 years, class of 1939 reunion
Hungary diplomatic dilemmas, Yale alumni seminar
Franklin Delano Roosevelt, Yale alumni seminar
Pacific security, Association of Yale Assembly San Francisco
Historical perception, influence on foreign policy
Review of Auside, the US-Israel alliance

title.part: Haiti under the Monroe Doctrine and today

did.unittitle: Open door and American foreign policy

title.part: Bosnia: Can we escape a return to the 1930s?

did.unittitle: Association of Professional Schools of International Affairs
Yale and the External World, Yale School of Medicine
Major Global Transformations of the 20th century Influencing Higher Education, Future Event Conference, Canberra
Intelligence, Morals, and Politics, Yale Espionage Conference
Review of Kai Bird, Color of Truth for Books Sandwiched In
Notes for talk to class of 1955 45th reunion
Remarks to the Yale Club of New Haven
Archivists talk, New England Archivists newsletter
Comments on A Problem from Hell by Samantha Powers
Whatever Happened to In Loco Parentis, Yale Club of NY, Class of 1948
Freedom of Expression talk, Association of Yale Assembly
John Gould's Eight Months Before the Mast, Mystic, CT
Strange Career of Free Expression, Yale Club of San Francisco
God and Man in Early New Haven, North End Club
Yale Center for International and Area Studies panel
Jim Lehrer Show panel on the occupation of Iraq
Association of Yale Assembly, Intelligence and Outbreaks of War
Graduate School Teaching Center, Spring forum
Yale leaders, Yale Club of New York
Class of '63 reunion in Washington, D.C.
The Birth and Fate of the Wilsonian Vision
Would Be Admiral: FDR and the Sea

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research, correspondence, drafts, administrative files related to published and unpublished writings by Gaddis Smith.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: I. Short stories and memoirs, 2003-2004. II. Academic, 1971-2005. Material arranged chronologically therein.

did.unittitle: Moving the Sources of Energy: A Theme for American Maritime History

title.part: Black Seamen and the Federal Courts

did.unittitle: Whatever Happened to National Self Determination?
